content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The changes implement the task prompt contract cleanly. The core contract (marker, section titles, versioning, validation) in task_prompt_contract.rs is correct and well-tested. Consumer guidance is properly gated behind `prompt_uses_contract()` checks across all four consumer sites (engine, completion, final_review, prompt_review). The prompt-review path correctly validates the refined prompt shape post-refinement when the original used the contract. Template integration uses optional placeholders with proper empty-string fallback and `collapse_blank_lines` cleanup. The `already_planned_elsewhere` population from `bead.dependents` (including `parent_child` kind) is confirmed intentional by the CLI integration test fixture. The repo_guidance formatting change from bullet-prefixed to verbatim multi-line is a correct fix validated by the new `render_bead_task_prompt_preserves_multiline_agents_guidance_verbatim` test. All 136 relevant tests pass. One minor naming issue flagged in the validator function.

## Amendments

1. In `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` (task_prompt_contract.rs:131), the variable `headings` is misleading — it contains ALL trimmed lines, not just headings. Rename to `trimmed_lines` or `lines` for clarity, since this function is part of the public validation contract and will be read by future maintainers.
   Rationale: The variable named `headings` is assigned `prompt.lines().map(str::trim).collect()` which collects every line in the prompt, not just headings. While the logic is correct, the name creates a false mental model for anyone reading the validator — they might assume only heading lines are being checked, when in fact the full-text line positions determine ordering validation. This is a maintainability concern in a public validation function that downstream consumers will rely on.
